req.title: Allergy intolerance (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall record an AllergyIntolerance linked to the patient; subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-084 requires: (1) a clinician can POST a valid AllergyIntolerance and get 201 + AuditEvent (positive/EP); (2) a duplicate POST returns 409 (negative/NEG); (3) a MedicationRequest for a substance matching the stored allergy triggers a conflict warning (state transition/STC); (4) the allergy code field at its minimum valid length (1-char system code) is accepted (boundary/BVA); (5) a decision-table case covering the combination of missing required fields returns 422 (DT). Five tests satisfy the >=3 minimum and cover all required heuristics. Each test uses a fresh patient fixture to remain idempotent.

tests.name: test_create_allergy_intolerance_happy_path
preconditions: No prior AllergyIntolerance for this patient+substance
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with valid FHIR R4 payload referencing patient_id and substance code 372687004
Assert HTTP 201 and resource id in response
GET /AuditEvent?entity=AllergyIntolerance/<new_id> and assert at least one event recorded
expected: AuditEvent list is non-empty and action equals C
trace: R-HLT-084 / AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_allergy_intolerance_happy_path(clinician_headers, fresh_patient_id, base_url):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{fresh_patient_id}"},
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-clinical", "code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-verification", "code": "confirmed"}]},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct", "code": "372687004", "display": "Amoxicillin"}]}
    }
    r = requests.post(f"{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload, headers=clinician_headers)
    assert r.status_code == 201, r.text
    allergy_id = r.json()["id"]
    assert r.json()["resourceType"] == "AllergyIntolerance"

    audit_r = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"AllergyIntolerance/{allergy_id}"},
        headers=clinician_headers
    )
    assert audit_r.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_r.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1
    actions = [e["resource"]["action"] for e in entries]
    assert "C" in actions

tests.name: test_duplicate_allergy_returns_409
preconditions: An AllergyIntolerance for the same patient+substance already exists (seeded by fixture)
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with identical patient reference and substance code as the pre-existing record
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with issue code duplicate
trace: R-HLT-084 / error pathway: 409 when exact duplicate
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_duplicate_allergy_returns_409(clinician_headers, fresh_patient_id, base_url):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{fresh_patient_id}"},
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-clinical", "code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-verification", "code": "confirmed"}]},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct", "code": "372687004", "display": "Amoxicillin"}]}
    }
    first = requests.post(f"{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload, headers=clinician_headers)
    assert first.status_code == 201, f"Seed failed: {first.text}"

    second = requests.post(f"{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload, headers=clinician_headers)
    assert second.status_code == 409, second.text
    body = second.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    codes = [i.get("code") for i in body.get("issue", [])]
    assert "duplicate" in codes

tests.name: test_medication_request_triggers_conflict_warning
preconditions: AllergyIntolerance for substance 372687004 (Amoxicillin) already stored for patient (state: allergy active)
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance for the patient with Amoxicillin to establish state
POST /MedicationRequest referencing same patient and medication code 372687004
Assert response contains a detectedIssue or warning indicating conflict
Assert AuditEvent emitted for the MedicationRequest operation
expected: Response or detectedIssue endpoint indicates allergy conflict warning
AuditEvent recorded for the MedicationRequest with action C
trace: R-HLT-084 / subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict
R-HLT-084 / AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_medication_request_triggers_conflict_warning(clinician_headers, fresh_patient_id, base_url):
    allergy_payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{fresh_patient_id}"},
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-clinical", "code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-verification", "code": "confirmed"}]},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct", "code": "372687004"}]}
    }
    ar = requests.post(f"{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance", json=allergy_payload, headers=clinician_headers)
    assert ar.status_code == 201, ar.text

    med_payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{fresh_patient_id}"},
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {"coding": [{"system": "http://snomed.info/sct", "code": "372687004", "display": "Amoxicillin"}]}
    }
    mr = requests.post(f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest", json=med_payload, headers=clinician_headers)
    assert mr.status_code in (200, 201), mr.text
    mr_id = mr.json()["id"]

    di = requests.get(f"{base_url}/DetectedIssue", params={"patient": fresh_patient_id, "implicated": f"MedicationRequest/{mr_id}"}, headers=clinician_headers)
    assert di.status_code == 200
    assert len(di.json().get("entry", [])) >= 1, "Expected allergy conflict DetectedIssue"

    audit_r = requests.get(f"{base_url}/AuditEvent", params={"entity": f"MedicationRequest/{mr_id}"}, headers=clinician_headers)
    assert len(audit_r.json().get("entry", [])) >= 1

tests.name: test_allergy_with_minimal_single_char_code
preconditions: No prior AllergyIntolerance for this patient
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with a substance code value of exactly 1 character (minimum length)
Assert HTTP 201 or 422 according to FHIR code min-length specification
expected: HTTP 201 if 1-char code is valid per FHIR R4 code datatype (min length 1)
Response resourceType is AllergyIntolerance on success
trace: R-HLT-084 / AuditEvent emitted for every operation
BVA: FHIR code datatype minimum length boundary = 1
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_allergy_with_minimal_single_char_code(clinician_headers, fresh_patient_id, base_url):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "AllergyIntolerance",
        "patient": {"reference": f"Patient/{fresh_patient_id}"},
        "clinicalStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-clinical", "code": "active"}]},
        "verificationStatus": {"coding": [{"system": "http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/allergyintolerance-verification", "code": "confirmed"}]},
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://example.org/custom", "code": "X"}]}
    }
    r = requests.post(f"{base_url}/AllergyIntolerance", json=payload, headers=clinician_headers)
    assert r.status_code in (201, 422), f"Unexpected status: {r.status_code} {r.text}"
    if r.status_code == 201:
        allergy_id = r.json()["id"]
        assert r.json()["resourceType"] == "AllergyIntolerance"
        audit_r = requests.get(
            f"{base_url}/AuditEvent",
            params={"entity": f"AllergyIntolerance/{allergy_id}"},
            headers=clinician_headers
        )
        assert len(audit_r.json().get("entry", [])) >= 1
    else:
        issues = r.json().get("issue", [])
        assert any(i.get("severity") in ("error", "fatal") for i in issues)

tests.name: test_missing_required_fields_returns_422
actions: POST /AllergyIntolerance with payload missing both 'patient' and 'code' required fields
Assert OperationOutcome lists issues for missing fields
expected: OperationOutcome with at least one issue of severity error
No AuditEvent of type C created (invalid operation not persisted)
trace: DT: decision table row — patient=absent, code=absent => validation failure
